author.purpose: Name heading each committed message. Largest type in the system; under review as louder than the minimal target.

mono.purpose: Code, inline code, and machine identifiers only.

motion.name: duration-state
motion.purpose: Recommended default for state transitions (hover, focus, selection). Target band 150-250ms.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Recommended ease-out-quart for state changes. No bounce, no elastic.

components.name: Panel
components.description: Structural frame for a screen region (chat list, transcript, participants, composer). Flat, bordered, never a stackable card.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: #161b22; border: 1px solid #30363d; border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px; color: #c9d1d9; }

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single filled action (Send). Dark text on Signal Blue. The only blue, filled button on any screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #58a6ff; color: #0d1117; font-weight: 600; border: none; border-radius: 6px; padding: 10px 20px; cursor: pointer; transition: filter 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.1); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #58a6ff; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Composer Input
components.description: Vertically resizable message textarea. Recessed below its surrounding panel (canvas fill), border shifts to accent on focus.
components.css: .ds-input { background: #0d1117; color: #c9d1d9; border: 1px solid #30363d; border-radius: 6px; padding: 10px 12px; font-family: inherit; font-size: 15px; line-height: 1.5; min-height: 80px; resize: vertical; outline: none; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #8b949e; } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: #58a6ff; }

components.name: Agent Stream Item
components.description: Signature component: ephemeral live narration/tool output for one working agent. Reads as provisional — dimmed and quieter than committed messages. Rewritten here without the banned side-stripe.
components.html:
Claude is working…
Reading messages_storage.py
🔧 grep "def get_response"
components.css: .ds-stream { opacity: 0.7; font-style: italic; margin: 0.25rem 0; padding: 8px 12px; background: rgba(255,255,255,0.02); border: 1px solid #21262d; border-radius: 6px; color: #c9d1d9; } .ds-stream-author { font-weight: 600; font-style: normal; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-stream-line { font-size: 0.85em; white-space: pre-wrap; }

narrative.overview: Manylogue is a room where several minds work at once and you watch the conversation settle. Many agents are active, but the space stays calm: muted ink on a near-black canvas, surfaces that recede, a single blue that lights up only what matters right now. It belongs to the lineage of terminal-adjacent agent tools (Claude Desktop, Codex, OpenCode) and should feel familiar to anyone who lives in them, then earn a second look through restraint and finish, not decoration. The system is flat and tonal: depth from three steps of near-black, never shadow; one system sans for UI, mono only for machine output; the accent rare by doctrine. Its job is to make the multi-agent state legible, then disappear into the task.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black canvas, three-step tonal depth, zero shadows at rest.
One system-sans family; mono only for code and agent/tool identifiers.
A single blue accent, used on roughly ≤10% of any screen.
Calm under constant streaming: nothing jumps, nothing shouts.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Signal Blue is the only chroma in the system and appears on ≤10% of any screen. If two things are blue, one of them is wrong. Status that is not act-here / focus-here / live-now is expressed in the neutral ramp, not in color.

rules.name: The Token-Only Rule
rules.body: Every color is one of the ten tokens. Raw hex in a component (the stray #ccc and #f8f8f2 in today's CSS) is a defect, not a shade.

rules.name: The Mono-Means-Machine Rule
rules.body: Monospace signals literal machine output: code, tool names, identifiers. Never use it for chrome or prose to look technical; the sans already carries that.

rules.name: The Flat Room Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Depth is tonal, never a shadow. The only license for a shadow is a genuinely floating layer (dropdown, dialog), and then one restrained soft shadow as a response to elevation.

dos: Do build surfaces as flat panels: Surface fill (#161b22), 1px Hairline (#30363d), 8px radius. Depth is tonal, never a shadow.
Do reserve Signal Blue (#58a6ff) for primary action, focus, current selection, and live state — ≤10% of any screen.
Do keep one system-sans family for all UI; use mono only for code and machine identifiers.
Do give every interactive element a visible :focus-visible state. The audience navigates by keyboard.
Do keep motion to 150-250ms state transitions, each paired with a prefers-reduced-motion fallback.
Do make ephemeral agent output read as quieter and more provisional than committed messages.
donts: Don't use side-stripe borders — a border-left/right thicker than 1px as a colored accent. The current .agent-stream-item border-left: 2px solid #ccc is a live violation.
Don't drift toward a generic SaaS dashboard: no card grids, no gradient accents, no hero-metric blocks.
Don't drift toward an enterprise IDE: no overwhelming panels, dense toolbars, or settings sprawl.
Don't introduce gradient text or glassmorphism. This palette is flat and solid by doctrine.
Don't hardcode color outside the ten tokens (today's strays: #ccc, #f8f8f2).
Don't let one non-content element shout — the 32px message Author is louder than the minimal target.
Don't let chat-list names overflow or wrap; truncate with ellipsis inside the fixed column.
